@@ -0,0 +1,176 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a680
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Fix: double-click on perspective tab opens inspector ŌĆö perspective is NOT an entity'
+---
+## What
+
+Double-clicking a perspective tab opens the inspector. It should not. **Perspective is not an entity** ŌĆö perspectives are saved view-state (filter + group + sort) attached to a board, not first-class inspectable objects. The inspector exists to show details of entities (board, column, task, tag, field), so dispatching `ui.inspect` against a `perspective_tab:<id>` moniker is an architectural mismatch.
+
+The user reports the symptom for the perspective tab, but the root cause is broader: every UI-chrome focusable currently inherits the same incorrect behavior.
+
+### Why it's broken
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx` (and the symmetric `focus-zone.tsx`) wires an unconditional double-click handler:
+
+```tsx
+// inside SpatialFocusScopeBody
+const dispatch = useDispatchCommand("ui.inspect");
+ŌĆ”
+const handleDoubleClick = useCallback((e: React.MouseEvent) => {
+  if (!handleEvents) return;
+  // ŌĆ”input/textarea/contenteditable guardsŌĆ”
+  e.stopPropagation();
+  dispatch({ target: moniker }).catch(console.error);
+}, [dispatch, moniker, handleEvents]);
+ŌĆ”
+<div ŌĆ” onDoubleClick={handleDoubleClick}>
+```
+
+So **every** `<FocusScope>` / `<FocusZone>` wrapper, regardless of what it represents, fires `ui.inspect` with its own moniker as the target on double-click. The set of focusables today includes a lot of pure UI chrome that does not correspond to an inspectable entity:
+
+- `perspective_tab:<id>` ŌĆö the bug the user reported.
+- `ui:navbar`, `ui:navbar.board-selector`, `ui:navbar.inspect`, `ui:navbar.search` ŌĆö nav-bar zones and buttons.
+- `ui:toolbar.*` ŌĆö toolbar action groups and buttons.
+- `ui:perspective-bar`, `ui:perspective`, `ui:view`, `ui:board`, `ui:grid` ŌĆö container zones for layout, not entities.
+- `cell:<row>:<col>` ŌĆö grid cells, addresses inside an entity, not entities themselves.
+
+The perspective tab additionally has its own `<button onDoubleClick={onDoubleClick}>` handler that calls `startRename(p.id)`. The button does not call `e.stopPropagation()`, so the double-click bubbles up to the wrapping `<Focusable>` which fires `ui.inspect` ŌĆö both rename **and** inspect run from one user gesture. Visible result: the inspector opens.
+
+### The fix shape ŌĆö opt-in, not opt-out
+
+Inspect-on-double-click should be an **explicit opt-in** on `<FocusScope>` and `<FocusZone>` via a new prop `inspectOnDoubleClick?: boolean` defaulting to `false`. Only call sites that wrap an actual entity opt in.
+
+Why opt-in (not "skip when moniker prefix is in a UI list" / "skip when no `entity:*` scope claimed"):
+
+- The set of entity prefixes is owned by the kanban domain (`task:`, `tag:`, `column:`, `board:`, `field:`, `attachment:`). Hard-coding that list in a generic spatial primitive smears domain knowledge into UI infrastructure.
+- A scope-based runtime check is brittle ŌĆö the answer depends on whichever `CommandScopeProvider` happens to be the nearest ancestor, not on what *this* focusable represents. A perspective tab nested inside a board scope would still match `entity:board` and silently route inspect to the board.
+- Opt-in flips the contract from "inspect everything unless told otherwise" (the current bug) to "inspect only entity-bearing call sites that explicitly say so." That matches how the rest of the spatial primitives work (`showFocusBar`, `navOverride` ŌĆö explicit, not inferred).
+
+### Files in scope
+
+**Primitive contract change:**
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx`
+  - Add `inspectOnDoubleClick?: boolean` to `FocusScopeOwnProps`. Default `false` in the destructuring.
+  - In both `SpatialFocusScopeBody` and `FallbackFocusScopeBody`, gate the entire `dispatch({ target: moniker })` call behind that flag. When `false`, the `useDispatchCommand("ui.inspect")` hook should not be called either (so the registry walk for `ui.inspect` is not paid by every UI chrome wrapper).
+  - Update the file-level docstring section that describes the click contract.
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx`
+  - Symmetric change to `<FocusZone>` ŌĆö add the same prop with the same default and gating.
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focusable.tsx`
+  - If `<Focusable>` still exists as a transitional re-export shim of `<FocusScope>` per the in-flight architecture-fix task `01KQ5PP55SAAVJ0V3HDJ1DGNBY`, propagate the prop through. If `<Focusable>` already collapsed into `<FocusScope>`, this file is gone ŌĆö skip.
+
+**Entity call sites that must opt IN (`inspectOnDoubleClick`):**
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.tsx` ŌĆö task / tag cards (`task:<id>`, `tag:<id>`).
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` ŌĆö column zone (`column:<id>`).
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` ŌĆö only the board entity wrapper, NOT the `ui:board` chrome zone.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` ŌĆö field rows (`field:<entity>:<name>`).
+- Any other call site whose moniker is a real entity (search via `git grep` for `moniker={asMoniker("` and classify each hit).
+
+**UI-chrome call sites that must stay opt-OUT (explicitly verify, do not change):**
+
+- `nav-bar.tsx` ŌĆö all `ui:navbar.*`.
+- All toolbar wrappers ŌĆö `ui:toolbar.*`.
+- `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` ŌĆö `ui:perspective-bar` and `perspective_tab:<id>`. The button's own `onDoubleClick` continues to call `startRename`.
+- `perspective-container.tsx` ŌĆö `ui:perspective`.
+- `view-container.tsx` ŌĆö `ui:view`.
+- `grid-view.tsx` ŌĆö `ui:grid` and `cell:<r>:<c>`.
+- The board chrome `ui:board` zone (the *container*, not the board entity itself).
+
+**Architectural guard to extend:**
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-architecture.guards.node.test.ts` ŌĆö add a guard that greps `kanban-app/ui/src/` for every `moniker={asMoniker("ui:` or `moniker={asMoniker(\`perspective_tab:` etc. occurrence and asserts none of those call sites carry `inspectOnDoubleClick={true}`. Conversely, add a positive guard that every `entity-card`, `column-view`, `field` call site DOES carry the prop. The list of UI-chrome prefixes lives in the test as a `BANNED_INSPECT_PREFIXES` constant alongside the existing decoration guards.
+
+### What this task does NOT do
+
+- Does not redesign moniker prefixes. `perspective_tab:<id>` stays as a moniker ŌĆö it just won't trigger inspect.
+- Does not touch the YAML scope `entity:perspective` (that scope serves the perspective.* command palette + context menu and is unrelated to the inspector).
+- Does not change the right-click context menu ŌĆö context-menu commands are scoped per call site already; this task is only about double-click ŌåÆ inspect.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Double-clicking a perspective tab does NOT open the inspector. It only enters inline rename mode (existing `onDoubleClick ŌåÆ startRename` behavior on the inner button).
+- [ ] Double-clicking any `ui:*` zone or leaf (nav-bar buttons, toolbar buttons, perspective bar, view/grid/perspective container zones) does NOT open the inspector. `mockInvoke` is never called with a `ui_inspect`-equivalent invoke for those interactions.
+- [ ] Double-clicking a card (`task:<id>` or `tag:<id>`) DOES open the inspector ŌĆö existing behavior is preserved.
+- [ ] Double-clicking a column (`column:<id>`) DOES open the inspector for that column.
+- [ ] Double-clicking a field row DOES open the inspector for that field.
+- [ ] `<FocusScope>` and `<FocusZone>` accept a typed `inspectOnDoubleClick?: boolean` prop. TypeScript flags the prop with autocompletion.
+- [ ] When `inspectOnDoubleClick` is unset or `false`, `useDispatchCommand("ui.inspect")` is not called inside the body ŌĆö verified by a unit test that asserts the `command-scope` registry sees no lookup for `ui.inspect` from a chrome wrapper.
+- [ ] The architectural guard test in `focus-architecture.guards.node.test.ts` passes: every UI-chrome wrapper omits `inspectOnDoubleClick`, every entity wrapper sets it to `true`.
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` is green.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-commands` are green (no Rust changes expected, but verify nothing regressed).
+
+## Tests
+
+### Browser Tests (mandatory)
+
+Run under Vitest browser mode (`vitest-browser-react` + Playwright Chromium). They prove the new opt-in behavior end-to-end against real DOM, so the human user does not have to manually click around to verify.
+
+#### Test files
+
+1. `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.no-inspect-on-dblclick.spatial.test.tsx` (new) ŌĆö locks in the reported bug.
+2. `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.inspect-opt-in.spatial.test.tsx` (new) ŌĆö exercises the new prop.
+3. Extend `focus-architecture.guards.node.test.ts` (existing) ŌĆö source-level guard for the chrome/entity classification.
+
+#### Setup (for browser tests)
+
+- Mock `@tauri-apps/api/core` and `@tauri-apps/api/event` per the canonical pattern in `grid-view.nav-is-eventdriven.test.tsx` (`vi.hoisted` + `mockInvoke` + `mockListen` + `fireFocusChanged`).
+- Render components inside `<SpatialFocusProvider><FocusLayer name="test">ŌĆ”</FocusLayer></SpatialFocusProvider>` plus the providers each component requires.
+
+#### Required test cases
+
+**File 1 ŌĆö `perspective-tab-bar.no-inspect-on-dblclick.spatial.test.tsx`:**
+
+1. **Regression: dblclick on perspective tab does NOT inspect** ŌĆö render `<PerspectiveTabBar/>` with two perspectives. Double-click a tab. Assert: (a) `<InlineRenameEditor>` mounts (existing rename behavior preserved ŌĆö the button's own `onDoubleClick` still fires); (b) the `mockInvoke` call list contains zero entries whose first arg matches `/inspect/i` and zero dispatches that route to `ui.inspect`. The simplest assertion: spy on `useDispatchCommand` (or assert via the command-scope registry) that no `ui.inspect` dispatch was issued for `perspective_tab:*`.
+
+2. **Regression: dblclick on the perspective bar's wrapping zone does NOT inspect** ŌĆö double-click on the `ui:perspective-bar` zone background (whitespace inside the bar, not on a tab). Assert no `ui.inspect` dispatch.
+
+3. **Single-click still focuses** ŌĆö single-click on a tab still calls `mockInvoke("spatial_focus", { key })`. Confirms we did not regress the focus path.
+
+**File 2 ŌĆö `focus-scope.inspect-opt-in.spatial.test.tsx`:**
+
+1. **Default (no prop) ŌåÆ no inspect** ŌĆö render a bare `<FocusScope moniker="task:fake">` (no `inspectOnDoubleClick`). Double-click. Assert no `ui.inspect` dispatch.
+
+2. **`inspectOnDoubleClick={true}` ŌåÆ inspect fires** ŌĆö render `<FocusScope moniker="task:fake" inspectOnDoubleClick>`. Double-click. Assert `ui.inspect` was dispatched with `{ target: "task:fake" }`.
+
+3. **`inspectOnDoubleClick={false}` explicit ŌåÆ no inspect** ŌĆö same as default but with the prop set explicitly. Assert no dispatch (locks in the prop semantics).
+
+4. **No `useDispatchCommand("ui.inspect")` registration when opt-out** ŌĆö render a `<FocusScope>` with `inspectOnDoubleClick={false}` inside a test harness that records every `useDispatchCommand` call. Assert `"ui.inspect"` was never registered. Reason: avoid paying the registry walk + memoization cost for every chrome focusable.
+
+5. **Symmetric for `<FocusZone>`** ŌĆö repeat the four cases above against `<FocusZone>`.
+
+6. **Entity opt-in real-world: card** ŌĆö render `<EntityCard>` (or its column-view equivalent that wraps a card) for a task. Double-click. Assert `ui.inspect` was dispatched with `{ target: "task:<id>" }`.
+
+7. **Entity opt-in real-world: column** ŌĆö render a `<ColumnView>` whose wrapper sets `inspectOnDoubleClick`. Double-click on the column body whitespace (not on a card). Assert `ui.inspect` was dispatched with `{ target: "column:<id>" }`.
+
+8. **Inner button stops propagation contract** ŌĆö render `<FocusScope inspectOnDoubleClick><button onDoubleClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}>x</button></FocusScope>` and double-click the button. Assert no `ui.inspect` (the inner handler still owns the gesture). Documents the escape hatch.
+
+#### Source-level guard ŌĆö extend `focus-architecture.guards.node.test.ts`
+
+```ts
+const CHROME_PREFIXES = ["ui:", "perspective_tab:", "cell:"];
+const ENTITY_PREFIXES = ["task:", "tag:", "column:", "board:", "field:", "attachment:"];
+```
+
+- Walk every `*.tsx` source file under `kanban-app/ui/src/`.
+- For each `<FocusScope`, `<FocusZone`, or `<Focusable` element whose `moniker={asMoniker("<prefix>...")}` matches a CHROME prefix, assert the same JSX element does NOT contain `inspectOnDoubleClick`. This catches "someone added `inspectOnDoubleClick` to a nav-bar button."
+- For each element whose moniker matches an ENTITY prefix, assert the element DOES contain `inspectOnDoubleClick` (or `inspectOnDoubleClick={true}`). This catches "someone wrapped a new card type but forgot the opt-in." Allow a per-call-site `// inspect:exempt` comment in case there's a special-case entity not meant to be inspectable (extremely unlikely).
+- Test files are excluded from the walk so test fixtures with synthetic monikers don't trigger.
+
+### How to run
+
+```
+cd kanban-app/ui && npm test
+```
+
+Headless on CI. The CI workflow `.github/workflows/*.yml` already runs this command.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write failing test 1 in File 1 first (proves the reported bug), confirm it fails, then add the prop + flip the default in `focus-scope.tsx` / `focus-zone.tsx`, then opt in at every entity call site, then add the guard. Each step keeps the test suite stable.

changes.value: Fix: double-click on perspective tab opens inspector ŌĆö perspective is NOT an entity

## What

Double-clicking a perspective tab opens the inspector. It should not. **Perspective is not an entity** ŌĆö perspectives are saved view-state (filter + group + sort) attached to a board, not first-class inspectable objects. The inspector exists to show details of entities (board, column, task, tag, field), so dispatching `ui.inspect` against a `perspective_tab:<id>` moniker is an architectural mismatch.

The user reports the symptom for the perspective tab, but the root cause is broader: every UI-chrome focusable currently inherits the same incorrect behavior.

### Why it's broken

`kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx` (and the symmetric `focus-zone.tsx`) wires an unconditional double-click handler:

```tsx
// inside SpatialFocusScopeBody
const dispatch = useDispatchCommand("ui.inspect");
ŌĆ”
const handleDoubleClick = useCallback((e: React.MouseEvent) => {
  if (!handleEvents) return;
  // ŌĆ”input/textarea/contenteditable guardsŌĆ”
  e.stopPropagation();
  dispatch({ target: moniker }).catch(console.error);
}, [dispatch, moniker, handleEvents]);
ŌĆ”
<div ŌĆ” onDoubleClick={handleDoubleClick}>
```

So **every** `<FocusScope>` / `<FocusZone>` wrapper, regardless of what it represents, fires `ui.inspect` with its own moniker as the target on double-click. The set of focusables today includes a lot of pure UI chrome that does not correspond to an inspectable entity:

- `perspective_tab:<id>` ŌĆö the bug the user reported.
- `ui:navbar`, `ui:navbar.board-selector`, `ui:navbar.inspect`, `ui:navbar.search` ŌĆö nav-bar zones and buttons.
- `ui:toolbar.*` ŌĆö toolbar action groups and buttons.
- `ui:perspective-bar`, `ui:perspective`, `ui:view`, `ui:board`, `ui:grid` ŌĆö container zones for layout, not entities.
- `cell:<row>:<col>` ŌĆö grid cells, addresses inside an entity, not entities themselves.

The perspective tab additionally has its own `<button onDoubleClick={onDoubleClick}>` handler that calls `startRename(p.id)`. The button does not call `e.stopPropagation()`, so the double-click bubbles up to the wrapping `<Focusable>` which fires `ui.inspect` ŌĆö both rename **and** inspect run from one user gesture. Visible result: the inspector opens.

### The fix shape ŌĆö opt-in, not opt-out

Inspect-on-double-click should be an **explicit opt-in** on `<FocusScope>` and `<FocusZone>` via a new prop `inspectOnDoubleClick?: boolean` defaulting to `false`. Only call sites that wrap an actual entity opt in.

Why opt-in (not "skip when moniker prefix is in a UI list" / "skip when no `entity:*` scope claimed"):

- The set of entity prefixes is owned by the kanban domain (`task:`, `tag:`, `column:`, `board:`, `field:`, `attachment:`). Hard-coding that list in a generic spatial primitive smears domain knowledge into UI infrastructure.
- A scope-based runtime check is brittle ŌĆö the answer depends on whichever `CommandScopeProvider` happens to be the nearest ancestor, not on what *this* focusable represents. A perspective tab nested inside a board scope would still match `entity:board` and silently route inspect to the board.
- Opt-in flips the contract from "inspect everything unless told otherwise" (the current bug) to "inspect only entity-bearing call sites that explicitly say so." That matches how the rest of the spatial primitives work (`showFocusBar`, `navOverride` ŌĆö explicit, not inferred).

### Files in scope

**Primitive contract change:**

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx`
  - Add `inspectOnDoubleClick?: boolean` to `FocusScopeOwnProps`. Default `false` in the destructuring.
  - In both `SpatialFocusScopeBody` and `FallbackFocusScopeBody`, gate the entire `dispatch({ target: moniker })` call behind that flag. When `false`, the `useDispatchCommand("ui.inspect")` hook should not be called either (so the registry walk for `ui.inspect` is not paid by every UI chrome wrapper).
  - Update the file-level docstring section that describes the click contract.

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx`
  - Symmetric change to `<FocusZone>` ŌĆö add the same prop with the same default and gating.

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focusable.tsx`
  - If `<Focusable>` still exists as a transitional re-export shim of `<FocusScope>` per the in-flight architecture-fix task `01KQ5PP55SAAVJ0V3HDJ1DGNBY`, propagate the prop through. If `<Focusable>` already collapsed into `<FocusScope>`, this file is gone ŌĆö skip.

**Entity call sites that must opt IN (`inspectOnDoubleClick`):**

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.tsx` ŌĆö task / tag cards (`task:<id>`, `tag:<id>`).
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` ŌĆö column zone (`column:<id>`).
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` ŌĆö only the board entity wrapper, NOT the `ui:board` chrome zone.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` ŌĆö field rows (`field:<entity>:<name>`).
- Any other call site whose moniker is a real entity (search via `git grep` for `moniker={asMoniker("` and classify each hit).

**UI-chrome call sites that must stay opt-OUT (explicitly verify, do not change):**

- `nav-bar.tsx` ŌĆö all `ui:navbar.*`.
- All toolbar wrappers ŌĆö `ui:toolbar.*`.
- `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` ŌĆö `ui:perspective-bar` and `perspective_tab:<id>`. The button's own `onDoubleClick` continues to call `startRename`.
- `perspective-container.tsx` ŌĆö `ui:perspective`.
- `view-container.tsx` ŌĆö `ui:view`.
- `grid-view.tsx` ŌĆö `ui:grid` and `cell:<r>:<c>`.
- The board chrome `ui:board` zone (the *container*, not the board entity itself).

**Architectural guard to extend:**

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-architecture.guards.node.test.ts` ŌĆö add a guard that greps `kanban-app/ui/src/` for every `moniker={asMoniker("ui:` or `moniker={asMoniker(\`perspective_tab:` etc. occurrence and asserts none of those call sites carry `inspectOnDoubleClick={true}`. Conversely, add a positive guard that every `entity-card`, `column-view`, `field` call site DOES carry the prop. The list of UI-chrome prefixes lives in the test as a `BANNED_INSPECT_PREFIXES` constant alongside the existing decoration guards.

### What this task does NOT do

- Does not redesign moniker prefixes. `perspective_tab:<id>` stays as a moniker ŌĆö it just won't trigger inspect.
- Does not touch the YAML scope `entity:perspective` (that scope serves the perspective.* command palette + context menu and is unrelated to the inspector).
- Does not change the right-click context menu ŌĆö context-menu commands are scoped per call site already; this task is only about double-click ŌåÆ inspect.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Double-clicking a perspective tab does NOT open the inspector. It only enters inline rename mode (existing `onDoubleClick ŌåÆ startRename` behavior on the inner button).
- [ ] Double-clicking any `ui:*` zone or leaf (nav-bar buttons, toolbar buttons, perspective bar, view/grid/perspective container zones) does NOT open the inspector. `mockInvoke` is never called with a `ui_inspect`-equivalent invoke for those interactions.
- [ ] Double-clicking a card (`task:<id>` or `tag:<id>`) DOES open the inspector ŌĆö existing behavior is preserved.
- [ ] Double-clicking a column (`column:<id>`) DOES open the inspector for that column.
- [ ] Double-clicking a field row DOES open the inspector for that field.
- [ ] `<FocusScope>` and `<FocusZone>` accept a typed `inspectOnDoubleClick?: boolean` prop. TypeScript flags the prop with autocompletion.
- [ ] When `inspectOnDoubleClick` is unset or `false`, `useDispatchCommand("ui.inspect")` is not called inside the body ŌĆö verified by a unit test that asserts the `command-scope` registry sees no lookup for `ui.inspect` from a chrome wrapper.
- [ ] The architectural guard test in `focus-architecture.guards.node.test.ts` passes: every UI-chrome wrapper omits `inspectOnDoubleClick`, every entity wrapper sets it to `true`.
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` is green.
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-commands` are green (no Rust changes expected, but verify nothing regressed).

## Tests

### Browser Tests (mandatory)

Run under Vitest browser mode (`vitest-browser-react` + Playwright Chromium). They prove the new opt-in behavior end-to-end against real DOM, so the human user does not have to manually click around to verify.

#### Test files

1. `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.no-inspect-on-dblclick.spatial.test.tsx` (new) ŌĆö locks in the reported bug.
2. `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.inspect-opt-in.spatial.test.tsx` (new) ŌĆö exercises the new prop.
3. Extend `focus-architecture.guards.node.test.ts` (existing) ŌĆö source-level guard for the chrome/entity classification.

#### Setup (for browser tests)

- Mock `@tauri-apps/api/core` and `@tauri-apps/api/event` per the canonical pattern in `grid-view.nav-is-eventdriven.test.tsx` (`vi.hoisted` + `mockInvoke` + `mockListen` + `fireFocusChanged`).
- Render components inside `<SpatialFocusProvider><FocusLayer name="test">ŌĆ”</FocusLayer></SpatialFocusProvider>` plus the providers each component requires.

#### Required test cases

**File 1 ŌĆö `perspective-tab-bar.no-inspect-on-dblclick.spatial.test.tsx`:**

1. **Regression: dblclick on perspective tab does NOT inspect** ŌĆö render `<PerspectiveTabBar/>` with two perspectives. Double-click a tab. Assert: (a) `<InlineRenameEditor>` mounts (existing rename behavior preserved ŌĆö the button's own `onDoubleClick` still fires); (b) the `mockInvoke` call list contains zero entries whose first arg matches `/inspect/i` and zero dispatches that route to `ui.inspect`. The simplest assertion: spy on `useDispatchCommand` (or assert via the command-scope registry) that no `ui.inspect` dispatch was issued for `perspective_tab:*`.

2. **Regression: dblclick on the perspective bar's wrapping zone does NOT inspect** ŌĆö double-click on the `ui:perspective-bar` zone background (whitespace inside the bar, not on a tab). Assert no `ui.inspect` dispatch.

3. **Single-click still focuses** ŌĆö single-click on a tab still calls `mockInvoke("spatial_focus", { key })`. Confirms we did not regress the focus path.

**File 2 ŌĆö `focus-scope.inspect-opt-in.spatial.test.tsx`:**

1. **Default (no prop) ŌåÆ no inspect** ŌĆö render a bare `<FocusScope moniker="task:fake">` (no `inspectOnDoubleClick`). Double-click. Assert no `ui.inspect` dispatch.

2. **`inspectOnDoubleClick={true}` ŌåÆ inspect fires** ŌĆö render `<FocusScope moniker="task:fake" inspectOnDoubleClick>`. Double-click. Assert `ui.inspect` was dispatched with `{ target: "task:fake" }`.

3. **`inspectOnDoubleClick={false}` explicit ŌåÆ no inspect** ŌĆö same as default but with the prop set explicitly. Assert no dispatch (locks in the prop semantics).

4. **No `useDispatchCommand("ui.inspect")` registration when opt-out** ŌĆö render a `<FocusScope>` with `inspectOnDoubleClick={false}` inside a test harness that records every `useDispatchCommand` call. Assert `"ui.inspect"` was never registered. Reason: avoid paying the registry walk + memoization cost for every chrome focusable.

5. **Symmetric for `<FocusZone>`** ŌĆö repeat the four cases above against `<FocusZone>`.

6. **Entity opt-in real-world: card** ŌĆö render `<EntityCard>` (or its column-view equivalent that wraps a card) for a task. Double-click. Assert `ui.inspect` was dispatched with `{ target: "task:<id>" }`.

7. **Entity opt-in real-world: column** ŌĆö render a `<ColumnView>` whose wrapper sets `inspectOnDoubleClick`. Double-click on the column body whitespace (not on a card). Assert `ui.inspect` was dispatched with `{ target: "column:<id>" }`.

8. **Inner button stops propagation contract** ŌĆö render `<FocusScope inspectOnDoubleClick><button onDoubleClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}>x</button></FocusScope>` and double-click the button. Assert no `ui.inspect` (the inner handler still owns the gesture). Documents the escape hatch.

#### Source-level guard ŌĆö extend `focus-architecture.guards.node.test.ts`

```ts
const CHROME_PREFIXES = ["ui:", "perspective_tab:", "cell:"];
const ENTITY_PREFIXES = ["task:", "tag:", "column:", "board:", "field:", "attachment:"];
```

- Walk every `*.tsx` source file under `kanban-app/ui/src/`.
- For each `<FocusScope`, `<FocusZone`, or `<Focusable` element whose `moniker={asMoniker("<prefix>...")}` matches a CHROME prefix, assert the same JSX element does NOT contain `inspectOnDoubleClick`. This catches "someone added `inspectOnDoubleClick` to a nav-bar button."
- For each element whose moniker matches an ENTITY prefix, assert the element DOES contain `inspectOnDoubleClick` (or `inspectOnDoubleClick={true}`). This catches "someone wrapped a new card type but forgot the opt-in." Allow a per-call-site `// inspect:exempt` comment in case there's a special-case entity not meant to be inspectable (extremely unlikely).
- Test files are excluded from the walk so test fixtures with synthetic monikers don't trigger.

### How to run

```
cd kanban-app/ui && npm test
```

Headless on CI. The CI workflow `.github/workflows/*.yml` already runs this command.

 ## Workflow

 - Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write failing test 1 in File 1 first (proves the reported bug), confirm it fails, then add the prop + flip the default, then opt in at every entity call site, then add the guard.
+
+## Implementation summary
+
+Implemented `inspectOnDoubleClick` opt-in prop on `<FocusScope>` / `<FocusZone>` via a small inner `<InspectDoubleClickRegistrar>` child component pattern: when the prop is true, the registrar mounts and calls `useDispatchCommand("ui.inspect")`, installing the dispatcher into a ref that the body's `onDoubleClick` reads. When false (the default), the registrar does not mount, the hook is never called, and `onDoubleClick` is not attached to the body div at all ŌĆö so the gesture bubbles to ancestors untouched.
+
+Entity call sites opted-in: `entity-card.tsx`, `column-view.tsx`, `board-view.tsx`, `fields/field.tsx`, `data-table.tsx` (entity-row scope), `mention-view.tsx` (mention pills), `fields/displays/attachment-display.tsx`. UI-chrome call sites stay opt-out by default (no code changes). The column-name navigation leaf in `column-view.tsx` carries `// inspect:exempt` because it wraps a synthetic `column:<id>.name` moniker whose inner `<Field>` zone owns the inspect opt-in.
+
+Architectural guard added to `focus-architecture.guards.node.test.ts`. Two new test files added: `perspective-tab-bar.no-inspect-on-dblclick.spatial.test.tsx` (4 tests) and `focus-scope.inspect-opt-in.spatial.test.tsx` (11 tests). Updated 2 existing tests in `focus-scope.test.tsx` to add the prop now that it's required.
+
+Full UI suite: 1714/1714 passing. Rust suites for `swissarmyhammer-kanban` and `swissarmyhammer-commands` also green.
